what_it_does: Lists absolute filesystem paths to design documents and code artifacts that together form the substrate for a pending migration pass (wizard run 1).
observations: Contains only a flat list of absolute paths; no code symbols or per-entry metadata.
Mixes Markdown design docs, TypeScript/MJS source and tests, SQL migrations, scripts, and generated schema files.
Heavy concentration on ElevenLabs-Wizard, elevenlabs-mcp package, vi-invariant, wizard-contracts, and wizard-edge-client.
Many paths appear twice: once under Visual-DNA-Vault/ElevenLabs-Wizard and again under packages/elevenlabs-mcp/vault/ElevenLabs-Wizard.
Includes discovery artifacts: direction cards, substrate audit notes, mechanism docs, framing docs, research briefs/outputs, and skill skeletons.
Contains system agent memory files, inbox/outbox/task notes, and a SUBSTRATE_LEDGER.md reference.
File is truncated at the end, cutting off a path in the _di directory.
standouts: The manifest treats design Markdown and code files as a single ledger of substrates.
Presence of an elaborate discovery framework with numbered direction cards and substrate audits.
Duplicated vault paths suggest a pending consolidation between an external vault and the package vault.
Includes vi-invariant as a separate package, indicating a cross-cutting invariant layer.
conventions: Uses absolute WSL-style paths starting with /mnt/c/.
Directory names use kebab-case and snake_case with numeric prefixes.
File extensions clearly separate design (.md) from code (.ts, .mjs, .sql, .py).
concerns: Truncated end means the manifest may be incomplete.
Duplicate entries without status annotations could cause confusion during migration.
No relative paths or root anchor makes portability unclear.
No per-entry kind or ownership metadata.
notes: This file is essentially a migration inventory: a long flat path list of every design doc and code artifact the wizard run considers in scope.
questions: What selection criteria determined which files are included or excluded?
Are the duplicated vault paths intended to be merged, or is one the source of truth?
What is the target location/package for the pending migration?
Is the truncation accidental or is the remainder stored elsewhere?
apparent_themes: Design-code co-evolution as a single substrate.
AI-native operating model and MCP-based tool integration.
Discovery-driven architecture with direction cards and substrate audits.
Wizard-mediated interface between ElevenLabs capabilities and a broader system.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to create a comprehensive, queryable inventory of all relevant design and implementation artifacts so a migration or fusion process can map, reconcile, and move them.
novelty: The ledger flattens high-level design documents, research outputs, direction cards, skill skeletons, and concrete code into one enumerated substrate, rather than separating docs from source.
contribution: Provides a broad, file-level map of the project's design and code surface that a fused system can use to trace artifact relationships, scope migration work, and avoid losing contextual documents.
summary_for_embedding: A flat manifest of absolute paths to ElevenLabs-Wizard design documents, elevenlabs-mcp and related package code, vi-invariant library files, SQL migrations, and discovery artifacts for a migration-pending wizard run.
intention_for_embedding: To enumerate all design and code substrates that need to be considered, reconciled, or migrated during wizard run 1.
